response.justification: The candidate is a versatile senior full-stack engineer with nearly 20 years of experience, strong testing culture, and some genuine high-scale systems work (low-latency services handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic). However, the role's hard minimum of 8 years of C++ is far from met — C++ appears only in a ~7-month stint at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis project, and it is absent from the skills list. His depth is in D, TypeScript, and Node.js rather than C++ and compute/storage infrastructure, and his leadership experience is mentoring via workshops and founding a small open-source project rather than leading technical strategy for a platform team. The profile diverges too much from the core requirements to justify an interview for this specific role.
strengths: Real large-scale, low-latency distributed systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, caching)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, plus teaching and workshop experience
Broad, long-tenured full-stack background with cloud exposure (AWS CDK, Kubernetes, Docker)
concerns: Does not meet the 8-year C++ minimum — C++ experience is under a year and not listed in current skills
No formal technical-leadership role owning platform strategy, priorities, and cross-team delivery
Primary depth is in D and TypeScript ecosystems, not cloud compute, storage, or hardware architecture

key_factors.factor: C++ experience far below minimum qualification
Limited formal technical leadership of platform teams
